channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Handyman named person of interest after missing woman's body found buried in concrete
item.description: A missing woman was found buried and partially encased in concrete more than a week after she went missing. Now, deputies are questioning her handyman for his possible role in her disappearance and death.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KTLA 5

item.title: San Francisco's new mayor is starting to unite the fractured city
item.description: Democrats nationally may be in turmoil, but liberals in San Francisco are hopeful over a new mayor's collaborative style in a city known for its infighting and chaotic streets.
item.dc:creator: Janie Har And Olga R. Rodriguez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DOGE targets a community service program in its latest cost-cutting effort
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — A 30-year-old community service program that sends young adults to work on projects across the U.S. was the latest target of the Trump administration 's campaign to slash government spending.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Proposed rule change on endangered species triggers alarm for environmentalists
item.description: The Trump administration plans to eliminate habitat protections for endangered and threatened species in a move environmentalists say would lead to the extinction of critically endangered species because of logging, mining, development and other activities.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Handyman named person of interest after missing woman's body is found buried in concrete
item.description: A missing woman was found buried and partially encased in concrete nearly two weeks after she went missing. Now, deputies are questioning her handyman for his role in her disappearance and death.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Colorado company to become first to refuel Space Force asset
item.description: Astroscale U.S. Inc., a Denver-based on-orbit services and logistics company, will perform two refueling operations of a U.S. Department of Defense satellite.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, NewsNation

item.title: MLB ump leaves game after foul ball hits him in the face
item.description: Veteran umpire Hunter Wendelstedt instantly hit the ground after he took a direct hit from the line smash off the bat of New York Mets center fielder Tyrone Taylor in the seventh inning.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with hundreds fearing deportation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At first, the bar association for immigration attorneys began receiving inquiries from a couple students a day. These were foreigners studying in the U.S., and they'd discovered in early April their legal status had been terminated with little notice.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, MAKIYA SEMINERA and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bryan Kohberger defense aims to create 'illusion' with renewed bid to toss evidence: fmr. FBI agent
item.description: A former FBI special agent says that Bryan Kohberger's defense team is trying to create an "illusion" for future jurors with a renewed effort to get some evidence tossed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about Hollywood icon Gene Hackman's and his wife's deaths
item.description: Authorities in northern New Mexico released police body camera video and other public records Tuesday in the investigation into the deaths of Gene Hackman and his wife, Betsy Arakawa. The two were found dead Feb. 26 in their Santa Fe home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FL representative says he'll go to El Salvador if wrongly deported man isn't returned this week
item.description: U.S. House Representative Maxwell Frost (D-Fla.) says he'll join other Democrat lawmakers planning to go to El Salvador if a man mistakenly deported isn't returned this week.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge rules against parents who wore wristbands to protest transgender student athlete
item.description: A federal judge ruled Monday that two fathers cannot wear pink wristbands with two X’s — the symbol for female chromosomes — drawn on them in opposition to a transgender high school athlete on one of their daughters’ rival soccer teams.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KRON4

item.title: Georgia man arrested over alleged threats to kill Tulsi Gabbard
item.description: A Georgia man was arrested after federal authorities said he made numerous death threats against Director of National Intelligence (DNI) Tulsi Gabbard, the Justice Department announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Man allegedly held captive by stepmother in Connecticut releases first public statement
item.description: In his first public statement, the man allegedly held captive in his home for over 20 years by his stepmother says he's been getting stronger as he begins the process of reclaiming his life.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, KRON4

item.title: Dodger Stadium, Universal Studios among venues for 2028 Los Angeles Olympics
item.description: LA28 officials have unveiled an even more detailed look at the venues that will be used during the 2028 Olympics.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump not expected to attend White House Correspondents' dinner
item.description: President Donald Trump is not expected to attend next weekend's White House Correspondents' dinner, The Hill confirmed on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: Why every MLB player will wear the same number Tuesday
item.description: Every MLB player, regardless of team or position, will be wearing the exact same jersey number on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Federal judge drops contempt case against ICE agent over arrest outside Boston courthouse
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge has dismissed a contempt case against an agent for the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement whose arrest of a man during his trial sparked criticism among the law enforcement community in Boston.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden says Trump administration has done 'breathtaking' damage to Social Security
item.description: Former President Joe Biden warned a Chicago audience assembled Tuesday at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled that the Trump administration has "taken a hatchet" to Social Security.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: IRS tax deadline extended in multiple states: What to know
item.description: Residents in all or parts of 13 states can file for an extension to allow disaster-affected areas more time to submit their taxes.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: College football trophy comes apart in Vance's hands at White House
item.description: The College Football Playoff National Championship Trophy came apart during a White House visit by the Ohio State Buckeyes football team Monday as Vice President JD Vance tried to pick it up.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, ABC 4

item.title: Shapiro arson attack underscores threats to politicians
item.description: The arson attack at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s (D) residence is the latest incident to underscore the threats faced by high-profile politicians amid the country’s political polarization.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, ABC 4

item.title: Starbucks introducing new, 'more consistent' dress code for baristas
item.description: Baristas at your local Starbucks will soon be sporting a new, more consistent look after the company announced a new dress code on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Former Colorado deputy gets 3 years in prison for fatally shooting man who called for help
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former Colorado sheriff’s deputy convicted of killing a 22-year-old man in distress who called 911 for help was sentenced Monday to three years in prison, the maximum sentence, by a judge who said the shooting was about power.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FEMA denies Washington state disaster relief from bomb cyclone, governor says
item.description: The Federal Emergency Management Agency has denied Washington state's request for emergency relief funds to help repair an estimated $34 million in damage from a deadly bomb cyclone storm system in November, according to Gov. Bob Ferguson.
item.dc:creator: LISA BAUMANN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Vance fumbles Ohio State's NCAA football championship trophy as Buckeyes visit the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President JD Vance ended the Ohio State football team's visit to the White House on Monday by fumbling the team's national championship trophy.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Reported intruder arrested near UnitedHealthcare headquarters months after CEO's killing
item.description: Police in the Minneapolis suburb of Minnetonka said the suspect was arrested outside of the United Healthcare corporate campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, Fox 5

item.title: Diddy pleads not guilty to additional charges in new indictment
item.description: The music mogul faces federal charges of racketeering, sex trafficking and of transportation to engage in prostitution.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, Fox 5

item.title: Stocks rally worldwide after Trump eases some of his tariffs on electronics, for now
item.description: Stocks rose worldwide Monday after President Donald Trump relaxed some of his tariffs, for now at least, and as stress from within the U.S. bond market seems to be easing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 5.2 magnitude earthquake shakes Southern California
item.description: The quake's epicenter was around two miles south of the community of Julian, about 60 miles east of San Diego.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, WGN 9

item.title: 5.2 magnitude earthquake strikes near San Diego
item.description: A powerful 5.2 magnitude earthquake rattled the San Diego County area Monday morning. According to the U.S. Geological Survey, the quake struck at 10:08 a.m. The epicenter was about 2.5 miles South of Julian, at a depth of roughly 8 miles.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he's open to sending violent criminals who are US citizens to El Salvador prison
item.description: President Trump on Monday indicated he would be open to sending American citizens who are violent criminals to El Salvador to be held in a notorious prison there, telling reporters his administration is studying law on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: A celestial 'smiley face' will be visible to stargazers later this month: What to know
item.description: Stargazers who turn their eyes skyward — and use a little bit of imagination — will have a chance to spot a rare "smiley face" in space later this month.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Key takeaways from the Bernie Sanders and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez rally in SLC
item.description: Thousands of Utahns filled the Jon M. Huntsman Center at the University of Utah on April 13 to listen to Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-VT) and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-NY) speak as part of Sanders' "Fighting Oligarchy" tour.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: US still won't say whether it will return mistakenly deported man, despite Supreme Court decision
item.description: The Trump administration is doubling down on its decision not to tell a federal court whether it has any plans to repatriate a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported last month and remains confined in a notorious prison in El Salvador, despite a Supreme Court ruling and lower court order that the
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4
